Petition On Behalf Of Robert C. Imperial For Attorney Fees And Costs Pursuant To The Memorandum Of Costs On Appeal
Off Calendar Apr-12-2022 Off Calendar

Petition On Behalf Of Robert C. Imperial For Attorney Fees And Costs Pursuant To The Memorandum Of Costs On Appeal
Off Calendar Feb-22-2022 Continued To Apr-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Stay And Distribution Of Undisputed Cash On Hand To Beneficiaries And Points And Authorities Thereon
Off Calendar Jun-14-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Albert Turnbaugh for Robert Imperial.
Janet Boessenecker for Public Administrator ("PA").
Susan Devermont appeared by telephone for American Research.

The Court noted that an appeal was filed. Mr. Turnbaugh stated that the estate is valued at over $450,000.00, that the amount in dispute is $63,867.43, that he is asking for distribution of everything but the $63,000.00 and that there is no prejudice to anyone. Ms. Boessenceker stated that the Public Administrator is neutral and that the PA can not do distribution without a Court order or a stipulation of the parties. Ms. Devermont stated that she has no objection as long as $63,867.45 is held back pending the outcome of the appeal. The Court stated that it may want to hold back more for the attorney fees. Mr. Turnbaugh stated that there is already a reserve of $75,000.00. Ms. Boessenecker suggested doing a preliminary distribution. The Court orders a hold back of $100,000.00 and doing preliminary distributions of $300,000.00. Mr. Turnbaugh is to prepare a form of order and have it approved as to form by all parties. The Court granted the Petition for Relief from Stay and Distribution of Undisputed Cash on Hand, etc. (subject to receiving a revised order reflecting modification of distribution).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Relief From Stay And Distribution Of Undisputed Cash On Hand To Beneficiaries And Points And Authorities Thereon
Off Calendar Apr-29-2019 Continued To May-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for San Francisco Public Administrator. Albert Turnbaugh for and with (Beneficiary) Robert Imperial. Guilio Accornero for American Research Bureau (ARB).

The Court stated that there are issues as to time slips for the amount of time spent by ARB on this matter. Mr. Accornero stated that this is a contingency fee case. Ms. Boessenecker addressed fee issues stating that they filed a 2nd supplement, that it states their position on statutory fees, that they did the full work and are entitled to statutory compensation, that the dispute of heirship lasted a year past their appointment, that if anyone had been appointed after there would have been an allocation of fees and extraordinary fees. Mr. Turnbough cited 10804 and that it has not been complied with, that they submitted points and authorities regarding the assignment, that ARB is seeking over 4 times the statutory fees and that it is excessive and should be denied. Mr. Accornero stated that the letter regarding the waiver is clear, that the Public Administrator has withdrawn their objections and had abandoned their search for heirs, that ARB found Mr. Imperial, that after all of the work is done he now complains about the fee, that ARB takes on contingency cases, that it is their only method of doing business, that many hours are spent and explained the work method and business practice. The Court stated that it understands his viewpoint regarding the time sheets but asked doesn't it make sense for the standpoint of the customer and the Court. Mr. Accornero replied that it would just be another cost and expense, talked about the cases that they spend time on but don't find anyone and cannot bill for, and indicated that 2 other firms had been brought in to find heirs at 15% but did not find Mr. Imperial. Mr. Turnbaugh stated that it is not appropriate to talk about all the time spent but not submit time sheets, that his client was told that he must ack quickly, that he had no time to talk to an attorney, that he is now subject to 25% in fees and that it is excessive, ludicrous and irrational. Mr. Accornero stated that it is fundamental contract law, ARB gets 25%. Ms. Boessenecker stated that regarding the Public Administrators fees dual compensation does not apply and cited 7622. The Court took the First and Final Report and Account, Petition for Fees, Petition for Final Distribution, Petition to Approve Assignments under submission.

Judge:Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Continued To Mar-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for petitioner San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Weinkauf stated that he had been waiting for letters of administration from Shasta County, which were finally received yesterday. He stated that he had submitted the probate status petition yesterday for efiling, and was awaiting acceptance of that filing. The matter was continued to March 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Shirley Le. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for petitioner San Francisco Public Administraor.

Mr. Weinkauf stated that one of the heirs had passed away, and the administrator was waiting for a probate of the heir's estate to be filed in Shasta County. The Court expressed concern regarding the delay in administration of the estate, and directed that a petition for approval of status of administration be filed. The matter was continued to March 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-13-2017 Continued To Feb-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Weinkauf requested a continuance stating that they have a deceased heir and that there will be a probate in Shasta County so that they can secure letters for their distribution. Status on close of estate is continued to February 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
